title: [NeuroQuest API] device-signature pass rate — signed-login success vs rejection
description:
Live view of the device-signature handshake pass rate (ok-vs-rejected) for the per-:id app-user endpoints.

Successful signed logins are SAMPLED (`device_signature:ok`, ~1% by default) while every rejection is logged in full, so a raw count comparison would massively understate success. Each tile recovers the true success volume by scaling the sampled count back up:

  scaled_ok = ok_count / sample_rate
  pass_rate = scaled_ok / (scaled_ok + rejected_count)

The scale-up factor uses `@extra.sample_rate` carried on every ok capture (NOT a hardcoded 0.01) so the math stays correct even after `DEVICE_AUTH_SUCCESS_SAMPLE_RATE` is changed. Pass rate is broken down by route (`@route`) and enforcement flag (`@extra.hard_mode`).

Signal source: captureMessage('device_signature:ok' | ':<status>') in artifacts/api-server/src/lib/deviceAuth.ts, shipped to Datadog Logs by artifacts/api-server/src/lib/errorMonitoring.ts when DATADOG_API_KEY is set.

definition.content:
## Device-signature pass rate

**pass_rate = (ok_count / sample_rate) / ((ok_count / sample_rate) + rejected_count)**

Successes are sampled (`device_signature:ok`, `@extra.sample_rate` per capture); rejections are always logged. The sampled successes are scaled back to true volume using the per-capture `sample_rate`, so this stays correct if `DEVICE_AUTH_SUCCESS_SAMPLE_RATE` changes. Rejections = `missing | invalid | expired | id_mismatch | issued_at_too_old | replayed` (excludes `no_server_key`, which is allowed through, not rejected).

search.query: service:neuroquest-api env:production ("device_signature:missing" OR "device_signature:invalid" OR "device_signature:expired" OR "device_signature:id_mismatch" OR "device_signature:issued_at_too_old" OR "device_signature:replayed")

service:neuroquest-api env:production ("device_signature:missing" OR "device_signature:invalid" OR "device_signature:expired" OR "device_signature:id_mismatch" OR "device_signature:issued_at_too_old" OR "device_signature:replayed")

service:neuroquest-api env:production ("device_signature:missing" OR "device_signature:invalid" OR "device_signature:expired" OR "device_signature:id_mismatch" OR "device_signature:issued_at_too_old" OR "device_signature:replayed")

service:neuroquest-api env:production ("device_signature:missing" OR "device_signature:invalid" OR "device_signature:expired" OR "device_signature:id_mismatch" OR "device_signature:issued_at_too_old" OR "device_signature:replayed")

definition.title: Pass rate broken down by route × hard_mode

search.query: service:neuroquest-api env:production ("device_signature:missing" OR "device_signature:invalid" OR "device_signature:expired" OR "device_signature:id_mismatch" OR "device_signature:issued_at_too_old" OR "device_signature:replayed")

_runbook.why: The sampled `device_signature:ok` capture now makes a true ok-vs-rejected ratio computable, but until now that ratio only existed implicitly in the log stream. This dashboard surfaces it as a single visible number plus trend lines so slow degradations (a pass rate drifting from 99.8% down over days) are obvious at a glance, complementing the spike-only device-signature-lockout monitor.
_runbook.math: scaled_ok = ok_count / sample_rate; pass_rate = scaled_ok / (scaled_ok + rejected_count). Successes are sampled so ok_count understates true success volume — scaling by 1/sample_rate recovers it. Implemented in Datadog formulas as 100 * (ok / rate) / ((ok / rate) + rejected).
_runbook.sample_rate_source: `rate` query = avg(@extra.sample_rate) over the device_signature:ok captures. Each capture emits @extra.sample_rate (the live DEVICE_AUTH_SUCCESS_SAMPLE_RATE, default 0.01, overridable). Using the emitted value — NOT a hardcoded 0.01 — keeps the scale-up correct after the env var is changed. @extra.sample_rate must be defined as a Datadog measure facet for the avg() to work; create it from a device_signature:ok log if it is not auto-detected.
_runbook.ok_signal: captureMessage('device_signature:ok', { route, extra: { hard_mode, sampled, sample_rate } }) in artifacts/api-server/src/lib/deviceAuth.ts — emitted on a Math.random() < SUCCESS_SAMPLE_RATE fraction of accepted signed requests.
_runbook.rejected_signal: captureMessage('device_signature:<status>', { route, extra: { hard_mode, active_install, reason } }). Rejections counted: missing, invalid, expired, id_mismatch, issued_at_too_old, replayed. no_server_key is EXCLUDED — that verdict is allowed through (treated as misconfig), not rejected, so it is not a login failure.
_runbook.breakdown: Grouped by @route (req.method + req.path) and @extra.hard_mode. In soft mode (hard_mode=false) nothing is actually rejected, so that series shows the pass rate the gate WOULD enforce if hard mode were on.
_runbook.import: Datadog dashboard JSON. Import via Datadog UI (Dashboards > New > Import dashboard JSON) or the Dashboards API (POST /api/v1/dashboard). Adjust env:production in the queries if viewing a non-prod env. Pairs with docs/monitors/device-signature-lockout.json (the paging alert).
